title: After return on line 552, lines 553-562 are unreachable dead code. The variable fr_trusted on line 553 is never defined (the old computation that defined it was removed in this refactor). The entire governance summary block (lines 556-562) is also dead code. python 552: return 553: if not fr_trusted: # <-- NameError: fr_trusted is not defined 554: console.print(...) 555: 556: # Governance summary ... # <-- all dead code 557: try: 558: from .issue_ledger import ledger_summary, render_governance_lines_cli 559: gov = ledger_summary(str(rd)) 560: except Exception: 561: gov = {} 562: console.print(render_governance_lines_cli(gov))
suggested_fix: Review the finding from model-review-raw.md and address it or mark it as accepted risk.

title: Run status is printed twice consecutively. python 543: console.print(f"[dim]Run status: {status}[/dim]") 544: console.print(f"[dim]Run status: {status}[/dim]")
suggested_fix: Review the finding from model-review-raw.md and address it or mark it as accepted risk.

title: On Windows, cmd_args = command and use_shell = True are set unconditionally, overriding the prior shlex.split() attempt. While is_shell_safe() provides a pre-check via policy patterns, this means no argument splitting happens on Windows and all commands run through shell interpretation. python 83: if sys.platform == "win32": 84: cmd_args = command 85: use_shell = True
suggested_fix: Review the finding from model-review-raw.md and address it or mark it as accepted risk.

title: The new evidence.py module and executor.py node populate safety_overall and forbidden_paths_touched in the state dict. These are used in finalizer.py, reviewer.py, run_governance.py, etc. However, they are not declared as fields in the WorkflowState Pydantic model.
suggested_fix: Review the finding from model-review-raw.md and address it or mark it as accepted risk.

title: The file grew from a small task list to 22,612 lines, predominantly containing duplicate test task entries (flaky batch, always fails, fails once, evidence gap, guard-test, idempotent-test) repeated across many timestamps.
suggested_fix: Review the finding from model-review-raw.md and address it or mark it as accepted risk.

title: D:\Tools\npm_pack\opencode is now the first candidate. On non-Windows platforms, _resolve_opencode_on_windows is not called, so this hardcoded Windows path is attempted as a literal pathname and fails, causing unnecessary subprocess.run call.
suggested_fix: Review the finding from model-review-raw.md and address it or mark it as accepted risk.

title: This shared module is called from cli.py, recover.py, and finalizer.py, but has no dedicated unit test. Test coverage is indirect via e2e and governance matrix audit tests.
suggested_fix: Review the finding from model-review-raw.md and address it or mark it as accepted risk.

suggested_fix: Check model reviewer output format and retry.

title: oracle_gate.py imports jsonschema at runtime (from jsonschema import validate, ValidationError). This package is not declared in pyproject.toml or any requirements file.
suggested_fix: Review the finding from model-review-raw.md and address it or mark it as accepted risk.

title: These absolute paths are machine-specific and break on any other system.
suggested_fix: Review the finding from model-review-raw.md and address it or mark it as accepted risk.

title: This file contains 27k lines of task definitions. It was added as a single new file (untracked → 23k additions).
suggested_fix: Review the finding from model-review-raw.md and address it or mark it as accepted risk.

title: _execute_run previously returned None and now returns dict[str, Any] ({"run_id": ..., "run_dir": ..., "status": ...}). Any internal caller depending on the None return will get unexpected behavior.
suggested_fix: Review the finding from model-review-raw.md and address it or mark it as accepted risk.

title: On Windows, every command runs with shell=True, bypassing shlex.split. While is_shell_safe() runs first as a gate, a regex-based safelist is less robust than argument-level injection prevention.
suggested_fix: Review the finding from model-review-raw.md and address it or mark it as accepted risk.

title: If jsonschema is not installed, the ImportError is caught by the broad except Exception, logged at warning, and returns False (schema validation failed → gate blocked).
suggested_fix: Review the finding from model-review-raw.md and address it or mark it as accepted risk.

title: Two hardcoded absolute Windows paths make the module environment-locked: python _DEFAULT_FLOW_OUTCOME_PATH = Path("D:/dev-frame-opencode/_reports/oracle-flow-state/s2/FLOW_OUTCOME.json") _SCHEMA_PATH = Path("D:/agent-acceptance/contracts/FLOW_OUTCOME.schema.json")
suggested_fix: Review the finding from model-review-raw.md and address it or mark it as accepted risk.

title: _write_execution_report does a delayed import from .execution_report_adapter import to_execution_report. There is no execution_report_adapter.py in the tracked changes and no evidence this module exists.
suggested_fix: Review the finding from model-review-raw.md and address it or mark it as accepted risk.

title: go_dispatch does from .task_spec_adapter import from_task_spec. Same risk as above — this module is not among new or modified files.
suggested_fix: Review the finding from model-review-raw.md and address it or mark it as accepted risk.

title: collect_safety_evidence imports from ..safety import produce_safety_report. The safety.py module is not among tracked changes.
suggested_fix: Review the finding from model-review-raw.md and address it or mark it as accepted risk.

title: The string "blocked" in fr_text.lower() can match incidental mentions (e.g., "This run was NOT blocked"). Similarly, "passed" can match "all tests passed" in a blocked run's error output.
suggested_fix: Review the finding from model-review-raw.md and address it or mark it as accepted risk.

title: python p0_count = unresolved_p0_count(run_dir) if p0_count > 0: status = "blocked" state["status"] = "blocked" state["review_result"] = "blocked" state["human_required"] = True state["error_message"] = f"Reviewer found {p0_count} unresolved P0 issue(s)"
suggested_fix: Review the finding from model-review-raw.md and address it or mark it as accepted risk.

title: python def _verification_commands_from_spec(verify: list[str]) -> dict[str, str]: commands: dict[str, str] = {} for index, command in enumerate(verify or [], start=1): if isinstance(command, str) and command.strip(): commands[f"verify_{index}"] = command.strip() return commands  If verify entries contain non-string objects (dicts with a cmd key, etc.), they are silently dropped with no warning. The TaskSpec format may allow structured verify entries.
suggested_fix: Review the finding from model-review-raw.md and address it or mark it as accepted risk.

title: is_shell_safe is now called inside run_command (lines 67-69), but _execute_run also calls it earlier in the pipeline (cli.py:1870). The same command is checked twice across different layers.
suggested_fix: Review the finding from model-review-raw.md and address it or mark it as accepted risk.

title: The candidate list now starts with r"D:\Tools\npm_pack\opencode", moving it from last to first position.
suggested_fix: Review the finding from model-review-raw.md and address it or mark it as accepted risk.

title: If to_execution_report returns an empty dict or raises, the function writes a mostly empty report file without warning the user.
suggested_fix: Review the finding from model-review-raw.md and address it or mark it as accepted risk.

title: A 24k-line YAML file has been added to tracked changes. This file likely contains task definitions. No obvious secrets were detected, but a file this large warrants explicit review for accidentally committed tokens or internal URLs.
suggested_fix: Review the finding from model-review-raw.md and address it or mark it as accepted risk.
